title: Settings
accessRestrictedMsg: Only administrators can access system settings.

help.title: Settings
help.intro: Global configuration for the entire Arthur MCP server. Changes saved here apply to all projects and all users — only administrators can access this page.
help.sectionServer: Server — the public URL of this instance and the default HTTP timeout for upstream API calls.
help.sectionSecurity: Security — JWT signing secret rotation for sessions, bearer tokens, and share links.
help.sectionHeaders: Global request headers — headers injected into outbound requests made by servers.
help.sectionEmail: E-mail (SMTP) — credentials used to send password-reset emails.
help.sectionObservability: Observability — environment variables that control logging, metrics, tracing, and OpenTelemetry settings.
help.saveReminder: Always click Save settings at the bottom after making changes. Navigating away without saving will discard your edits.
help.workflowReview: Open the relevant tab and review the current value before changing it; every setting on this page has instance-wide impact.
help.workflowSave: Enter the new value and click Save settings. Unsaved edits are discarded when you leave the page or switch tabs.
help.workflowVerify: Complete the verification described in that section's help after saving, such as connecting an MCP client or requesting a password reset.
help.cautionBody: Coordinate changes to URLs, credentials, headers, and signing secrets with users of this instance. A wrong value can interrupt every server, user session, or outbound request.

server.title: Server
server.urlHelper: Used to generate curl examples and password reset links.
server.helpIntro: These settings define how users and AI clients reach Arthur and how long Arthur waits for upstream APIs.
server.helpPasswordReset: Password-reset emails: when a user requests a password reset, Arthur sends an email with a link built as [your URL]/reset-password. If this URL is wrong or not reachable from the user's browser, the link in the email will be broken and the user will be unable to complete the reset. The most common mistake is entering an internal hostname or a localhost address.
server.helpMcpEndpoint: MCP endpoint construction: the Connect tab of each server builds the full MCP endpoint URL from this base, so users can copy the correct address and paste it into their AI client. If this base URL is wrong, users will copy an incorrect endpoint. Check this setting first if users report that their AI client cannot connect.
server.helpTimeout: Default timeout: maximum time Arthur waits for an upstream API call before returning a timeout error. A server-specific timeout takes precedence.
server.helpSetupUrl: Enter the public HTTPS origin users can reach, without a trailing slash, internal hostname, or path.
server.helpSetupTimeout: Choose a timeout that covers normal upstream response times while still failing stalled calls promptly.
server.helpSetupSave: Click Save settings before opening another tab.
server.helpVerify: Open a server's Connect tab and confirm its MCP URL uses this origin. Then run a representative Tool; slow calls should complete and stalled calls should time out as expected.
server.helpTimeoutTip: Changing the public URL makes previously copied MCP and password-reset URLs outdated. A timeout that is too low rejects legitimate work; one that is too high keeps AI clients waiting on stalled requests.

headers.title: Global request headers
headers.helpBody: Use global headers for values every upstream API request needs, such as a gateway identifier or shared tenant header. They apply to Tools, Resources, and Chains across all servers.
headers.helpAdd: Click Add header once for each name/value pair.
headers.helpEnter: Enter the exact HTTP header name and the value expected by the upstream API.
headers.helpSave: Click Save settings; rows with a blank name are ignored.
headers.helpNameEffect: Name identifies the HTTP header, for example X-Tenant-ID.
headers.helpValueEffect: Value is sent unchanged with each outbound request.
headers.helpOverrideEffect: A header configured on an endpoint or supplied by a Tool parameter overrides a global header with the same name.
headers.helpVerify: Run a Tool against a test endpoint and confirm the upstream service received the expected header and value.
headers.helpCaution: Do not place a server-specific credential here: it would be sent to every upstream API. Use server authentication or a Secret when only one server needs the value, and remove obsolete headers promptly.
headers.empty: No global headers defined — click Add header to set one

security.title: Security
security.helpIntro: The JWT secret proves that sessions and tokens were issued by this Arthur instance. Arthur uses the saved value instead of the environment value when one is configured here.
security.helpGenerate: Generate a long, random value with a trusted password or secret generator; do not reuse a human password.
security.helpEnter: Paste it into JWT secret. When a secret already exists, leave the field blank to keep it unchanged.
security.helpSave: Click Save settings once, then distribute replacement credentials only through approved secure channels.
security.helpVerify: Sign in again and confirm a newly issued session works. If MCP or OAuth clients use bearer tokens, reconnect one client and run a Tool.
security.helpRotation: Rotating this secret invalidates existing signed sessions and bearer tokens immediately. Users must sign in again, OAuth clients need fresh tokens, and active MCP connections using old tokens can fail. Plan rotation as a production credential change.
security.jwtSecretAlreadySet: JWT secret already configured — leave blank to keep it.
security.jwtSecretHelper: Use at least 16 characters. A long random value is recommended.
security.jwtSecretError: JWT secret must be at least 16 characters.

smtp.title: E-mail (SMTP)
smtp.passwordAlreadySet: SMTP password already set — leave blank to keep it.
smtp.helpIntro: SMTP lets Arthur deliver transactional email. It is currently used for password-reset messages; without a working configuration, users cannot receive their reset link.
smtp.helpHost: SMTP Host: the outbound mail server hostname provided by your email provider. Common examples: smtp.gmail.com (Gmail), smtp.office365.com (Microsoft 365), smtp.sendgrid.net (SendGrid), email-smtp.us-east-1.amazonaws.com (AWS SES). Use the exact hostname from your provider's documentation — a wrong host will cause a connection timeout.
smtp.helpPort: Port: the port your provider uses for outbound mail. Use 587 for STARTTLS (the most common modern option) or 465 for implicit SSL/TLS. Avoid port 25 — it is typically blocked by cloud hosting providers to prevent spam. If unsure, try 587 first.
smtp.helpUser: SMTP User: the username used to authenticate with the SMTP server. For most providers this is the full sender email address (e.g. noreply@company.com). For AWS SES it is an IAM access key ID, not an email address — check your provider's documentation.
smtp.helpPassword: SMTP Password: the authentication password for the SMTP user. For Gmail with two-factor authentication enabled, you must generate an App Password — your regular Google account password will be rejected. For SendGrid, use your API key here instead of a password.
smtp.helpFrom: From email: the sender address that appears in the recipient's inbox in the 'From' field (e.g. noreply@company.com or Arthur MCP <noreply@company.com>). This address must be authorised by your SMTP account — sending from an unauthorised domain will cause delivery failures or spam-folder routing.
smtp.helpSetupProvider: Create or choose a sender in your email provider and obtain its SMTP host, port, username, and password or app password.
smtp.helpSetupFields: Enter those credentials and an authorised From address. Use port 587 for STARTTLS or 465 only when your provider requires implicit TLS.
smtp.helpSetupSave: Click Save settings. Leave the password blank only when you intend to keep the already stored password.
smtp.helpVerify: From the login page, request a password reset for a test account. Confirm the message arrives, the sender is correct, and the reset link opens the public Arthur URL.
smtp.helpRotation: Treat the SMTP password as a production credential and rotate it if exposed. An unauthorised From address can be rejected or marked as spam. After any credential, domain, or port change, repeat the password-reset test.

saveValidationError: Please fix validation errors before saving.
